headlines.headline: Turkey Warns US Withdrawal from European Security Would Be 'Destructive'
headlines.summary: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan said discussions are underway on managing a possible US withdrawal from European security architecture, calling the potential move 'destructive' for regional stability.

headlines.headline: Iran Reimposes Restrictions on Strait of Hormuz, Vessels Report Gunfire
headlines.summary: Iran has reimposed 'strict control' on the Strait of Hormuz just one day after announcing its reopening, with shipping sources reporting at least two vessels came under fire as the waterway closed again.

headlines.headline: Saudi Finance Minister: Oil-Producing Countries Need Time to Ramp Up Output
headlines.summary: Saudi Finance Minister Mohammed Al-Jadaan welcomed Iran's reopening of the Strait of Hormuz but warned it will take time for oil-producing nations to increase output, noting the Middle East situation remains 'very fragile' until a durable de-escalation is achieved.

headlines.headline: The Iran War Has Revealed Trump's Pressure Point: The Economy
headlines.summary: Iran has taken a beating militarily but demonstrated it can exact economic costs that Trump and his aides underestimated, raising the political risk back home and creating a new dynamic in the conflict.

headlines.headline: Displaced Lebanese Return Home as Ceasefire Is Tested
headlines.summary: More displaced Lebanese are heading home as the ceasefire appears to mostly hold, though Israel says it carried out strikes on what it called 'terrorists' approaching the border area.

headlines.headline: Sanctioned Tankers Cross Strait of Hormuz Before Shots Fired
headlines.summary: Ship-tracking data shows at least three sanctioned vessels passed through the Strait of Hormuz before Iran reimposed restrictions and gunfire was reported at vessels in the waterway.

headlines.headline: Iranian Official Says US 'Maximalist' Demands Stall Face-to-Face Talks
headlines.summary: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister told the AP on the margins of a Turkey diplomacy forum that maximalist US demands are preventing face-to-face negotiations, with no date yet set for a new round of talks.

headlines.headline: Climate Now Embedded in Geopolitics and Energy Policy, Says Former COP30 Official
headlines.summary: Climate has moved beyond a 'niche theme' and is now embedded in geopolitics, energy, and global policy discussions, said a former COP30 official in an interview with Anadolu Agency.
